sections.label: Base Image
sections.aiHint: Defines the starting image for the Docker build. Every Dockerfile starts with a FROM instruction.

fields.label: Base image
fields.help: Always pin a specific tag. Prefer slim or alpine variants to reduce image size.
fields.aiHint: The base Docker image and tag e.g. node:20-alpine, python:3.12-slim, openjdk:21-jre-alpine, nginx:1.25.

fields.label: Multi-stage build
fields.help: Multi-stage builds separate the build environment from the runtime image, producing much smaller final images.
fields.aiHint: Whether to use a multi-stage build to separate build dependencies from the final runtime image.

fields.label: Build stage image
fields.help: Image used in the build stage. Only applies when multi-stage build is enabled.
fields.aiHint: The image for the build stage when using multi-stage builds e.g. maven:3.9-eclipse-temurin-21 for Java.

fields.label: Runtime stage image
fields.help: Minimal image for the final runtime stage. Should be smaller than the build stage image.
fields.aiHint: The minimal runtime image for the final stage e.g. eclipse-temurin:21-jre-alpine, nginx:alpine.

sections.label: Working Directory
sections.aiHint: Sets the working directory inside the container for all subsequent instructions.

fields.label: Working directory
fields.help: All subsequent COPY, RUN, CMD instructions are relative to this directory.
fields.aiHint: The working directory inside the container e.g. /app, /usr/src/app, /opt/app.

sections.label: Labels
sections.aiHint: Metadata labels applied to the image for documentation and tooling.

fields.label: Maintainer
fields.aiHint: Email or name of the team or person maintaining this image.

fields.label: Version
fields.aiHint: Version of the application in this image.

fields.label: Description
fields.aiHint: Short description of what this image contains.

sections.label: Environment Variables
sections.aiHint: Sets environment variables available during build and at runtime.

fields.label: Variable name
fields.help: Use UPPER_SNAKE_CASE for environment variable names.
fields.aiHint: Environment variable name in UPPER_SNAKE_CASE.

fields.label: Value
fields.help: For secrets, do not hardcode values here — inject them at runtime with docker run -e or via orchestration secrets.
fields.aiHint: Default value for the environment variable. Avoid hardcoding secrets here.

sections.label: Copy Dependency Files
sections.aiHint: Copies dependency manifest files before source code to maximise Docker layer caching.

fields.label: Dependency files (one per line)
fields.help: Copy dependency manifests before source code to leverage Docker layer caching. When only source code changes, the dependency install layer is reused.
fields.aiHint: Dependency manifest filenames to copy before source e.g. package.json and package-lock.json for Node, pom.xml for Maven, requirements.txt for Python.

sections.label: Install Dependencies
sections.aiHint: Runs the package manager install command to install application dependencies.

fields.label: Install command
fields.help: Use ci instead of install for reproducible builds. --only=production omits devDependencies.
fields.aiHint: The command to install dependencies e.g. npm ci, pip install -r requirements.txt, mvn dependency:resolve.

sections.label: Copy Source Code
sections.aiHint: Copies the application source code into the container image.

fields.label: Source path
fields.help: Use .dockerignore to exclude node_modules, .git, build artifacts, and sensitive files.
fields.aiHint: Source path to copy from the build context. Usually . for the entire project.

fields.label: Destination path
fields.aiHint: Destination inside the container. Usually . to copy into the working directory.

sections.label: Build Command
sections.aiHint: Runs the build step to compile or bundle the application e.g. npm run build, mvn package.

fields.label: Build command
fields.aiHint: The build command e.g. npm run build, mvn package -DskipTests, go build -o app .

sections.label: Additional RUN Commands
sections.aiHint: Executes shell commands during the image build e.g. creating directories, setting permissions, installing system packages.

fields.label: Command
fields.help: Chain related commands with && to minimise image layers. Clean up package manager caches in the same RUN instruction.
fields.aiHint: Shell command to run during build e.g. apt-get install -y curl, chmod +x /app/start.sh, adduser --system appuser.

sections.label: Expose Ports
sections.aiHint: Documents which ports the container listens on. EXPOSE is documentation only — actual port publishing requires -p in docker run or ports in Compose.

fields.label: Port
fields.aiHint: Port the application listens on inside the container.

fields.label: Protocol
fields.aiHint: Transport protocol. TCP is correct for HTTP/HTTPS and most application ports.

sections.label: Non-Root User
sections.aiHint: Creates and switches to a non-root user for security. Best practice for production images.

fields.label: Username
fields.help: Running as root inside a container is a security risk. Always use a non-root user in production.
fields.aiHint: Name of the non-root user to create and run the application as.

fields.label: UID
fields.help: Use a UID above 1000 to avoid conflicts with system users.
fields.aiHint: Numeric user ID. Use a value above 1000.

sections.label: Healthcheck
sections.aiHint: Defines how Docker checks if the container is healthy. Used by orchestrators like Compose and Swarm.

fields.label: Healthcheck command
fields.help: Command must return 0 for healthy, 1 for unhealthy. curl or wget are commonly used.
fields.aiHint: Command to check container health e.g. curl -f http://localhost:8080/health || exit 1.

fields.label: Interval
fields.aiHint: How often to run the healthcheck e.g. 30s.

fields.label: Timeout
fields.aiHint: How long to wait for the healthcheck to complete before marking it failed.

fields.label: Retries
fields.aiHint: Number of consecutive failures before the container is considered unhealthy.

sections.label: Entrypoint & CMD
sections.aiHint: Defines how the container starts. ENTRYPOINT sets the executable; CMD provides default arguments.

fields.label: Start type
fields.help: CMD only is simplest. ENTRYPOINT + CMD is best for production — ENTRYPOINT is the executable, CMD provides overridable args.
fields.aiHint: How to start the container. CMD only for simple cases. ENTRYPOINT + CMD for production.

fields.label: ENTRYPOINT
fields.help: The executable to run. Use exec form: node, java, python, nginx, etc.
fields.aiHint: The executable to run as the entrypoint e.g. node, java, python, nginx, /app/start.sh.

fields.label: CMD / arguments
fields.help: With ENTRYPOINT: arguments passed to the entrypoint. Without: the full command to run.
fields.aiHint: The command or arguments e.g. server.js, -jar app.jar, manage.py runserver, -g daemon off; for nginx.
